title: Market Brief — Wednesday, April 29, 2026
summary: GPU supply is bifurcated: A100s at 85% and H100s at 70% availability signal oversupply on legacy silicon, while B200s at 25% are scarce and GB200s at 10% face severe shortage. The H200 spot price surged 28.6% day-over-day to $3.00/hr on tightening availability of 45%, the largest single-day move across all GPU classes and a clear demand signal for next-gen memory bandwidth.
paragraphs: GPU supply is bifurcated: A100s at 85% and H100s at 70% availability signal oversupply on legacy silicon, while B200s at 25% are scarce and GB200s at 10% face severe shortage. The H200 spot price surged 28.6% day-over-day to $3.00/hr on tightening availability of 45%, the largest single-day move across all GPU classes and a clear demand signal for next-gen memory bandwidth.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), serving Llama 70B on H100s runs $9.84/M tokens, meaning DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.378/M output is selling at a loss of roughly $9.46 per million tokens. DeepSeek: Burning Cash. OpenAI charges premium-tier pricing on o3 Pro ($80/M output) with 83-95% margins on B200s: Strong. Anthropic splits the middle with Haiku at $5/M undercutting Opus at $25/M: Neutral. Google: Neutral. Meta and xAI at sub-$0.60/M output: Burning Cash.

B200 spot at $4.20/hr is undervalued given 25% availability, surging utilization up 33.9pp in 30 days, and the only GPU class delivering positive margins at mid-tier pricing. H200 is overvalued after its 28.6% spike with utilization actually declining 3.2pp monthly, making it a short candidate against long B200 exposure.
bullets: 25% B200 availability qualifies as scarce compute, with spot range already $3.50-$5.50/hr.
10% GB200 availability marks a severe shortage with no reliable spot pricing yet established.
$3.00/hr H200 spot represents a 28.6% overnight surge, the steepest GPU price move in today's data.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means API pricing has fallen roughly 79% since GPT-4 launch.
$0.378/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 is 99.4% cheaper than the $60/M benchmark at GPT-4 launch.
79.1% B200 utilization jumped 33.9 percentage points in 30 days, the fastest ramp of any GPU class.
